insights.title: Monophonic Harmonic Filter with >1 bands produces silence
insights.summary: Using the monophonic version of the Harmonic Filter with band count set to more than 1 results in complete silence. The polyphonic version does not exhibit this issue. Reported as still unresolved.
insights.verify_hint: grep MasterEffectProcessor or monophonic path in HarmonicFilter.cpp; check processSamples for non-VoiceEffect path

insights.title: Monophonic Harmonic Filter can kill system audio on Linux/JACK
insights.summary: Adding the monophonic Harmonic Filter to a project can silence all audio system-wide on Linux with JACK — not just HISE output, but other apps too. The problem only occurs with the monophonic version and only when band count > 1.
insights.verify_hint: grep JACK or audio device initialisation in HISE core; check if monophonic HarmonicFilter has an uninitialized buffer or divide-by-zero on Linux

insights.title: FilterDisplay FloatingTile cannot display the Harmonic Filter
insights.summary: The FilterDisplay content type does not support the Harmonic Filter. The SliderPack components (A, B, Mix) must be promoted to the interface and placed manually on the panel to build a UI for this module.

insights.title: Cannot apply Harmonic Filter per-voice on a Sampler
insights.summary: The Harmonic Filter operates on a single synthesiser voice. There is no way to insert it so that it filters individual sampler voices differently — it can only be placed in the FX chain where it processes the mixed output or a single voice at a time.

insights.title: SliderPack components can be promoted to the interface
insights.summary: The three internal SliderPack components (A, B, Mix) can be promoted to the plugin interface via the normal HISE interface builder workflow. This allows custom colouring and layout. Prior to a 2018 fix, parameter IDs were missing and promotion was not supported.

insights.title: Polyphonic version is significantly more CPU-intensive than monophonic
insights.summary: The polyphonic Harmonic Filter scales linearly with voice count — every active voice runs the full filter bank independently. The author has confirmed it is 'quite a CPU hog, especially in the polyphonic version'. With 16 bands and high polyphony, CPU cost can be prohibitive.
